address_range.note: One parcel: 48 is the front building, 46 the rear cottage (renumbered from 48A in 2005).

historic_status.reason: No evaluation and 45 years or older

events.event: Standing on the west side of Mars in a photograph from Kite Hill (OpenSFHistory wnp27.6091).
Visible above the earthquake refugee camp at Corbett and Mars (OpenSFHistory wnp37.00189).

building_history.conflict: The assessor records 1903; the c. 1895 photograph shows the house already standing.

permits.description: Front building, unit 48: comply with physical inspection report CC-5548.
Repair front stairs and railing, less than 50%.
Interior remodel within existing building envelope; new doors and windows to rear building.
Correct microfilm record: existing rear cottage of one room and one bathroom.
Repair dry rot and termite damage.
Rebuild front stairs to match; repair dry rot.

sources.name: Corbett Heights Neighbors, "January Neighborhood News", Vol. VIII, No. 1 (1 January 2026)
sources.cites: OpenSFHistory photograph wnp27.6091 (c. 1895, Mars Street from Kite Hill)

sources.name: Corbett Heights Neighbors, "April Neighborhood News", Vol. VIII, No. 4 (1 April 2026)
sources.cites: OpenSFHistory photograph wnp37.00189 (April 1906, the refugee camp at the Slope) and a 1912 photograph in the San Francisco History Center, San Francisco Public Library
